name: Sierra de Fuentes and Guadiloba Reservoir Loop
name_original: Sierra de Fuentes desde Cáceres – circular por la antigua Mina de Valdeflores y el río Gualdiloba
description: This difficult 24.9-mile touring cycling route from Cáceres explores Mediterranean forests, the old Valdeflores Mine, and the Río Gualdiloba
region_intro: Touring cycling around Cáceres offers diverse landscapes, from high mountains and river valleys to Mediterranean forests and unique granite rock formations. The region is characterized by extensive oak forests and ancient Roman roads, providing varied terrain for cyclists. These routes traverse areas with significant natural features, including UNESCO Biosphere Reserves and natural monuments. The terrain includes plains, hills, and river valleys, offering a range of experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Cáceres The most popular touring cycling route is Malpartida de Cáceres from Cáceres – circular by Monumento Natural Los Barruecos, a 21.6 miles (34.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 49 minutes to complete. This route explores a unique landscape of granite formations and ponds. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vía de la Plata and Embalse del Casar Loop, a moderate 34.5 miles (55.5 km) path. This route follows a historic Roman road and passes by the Embalse del Casar reservoir. Local touring cyclists also love the Sierra de Fuentes and Guadiloba Reservoir Loop, a 24.9 miles (40.1 km) trail leading through hilly terrain and past the Guadiloba Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 19 minutes. Touring cycling around Cáceres is defined by river valleys, granite formations, and extensive oak forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy city loops to more challenging routes through the sierras. The routes in Cáceres are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 900 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cáceres's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Cáceres?
A: Cáceres offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 90 options available for exploration. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy city loops to more challenging rides through the sierras.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in Cáceres?
A: Cáceres features diverse terrain, including high mountains, river valleys, Mediterranean forests, dramatic gorges, and unique granite rock formations. You'll also find extensive oak forests and ancient Roman roads, providing varied and engaging rides.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes for beginners in Cáceres?
A: Yes, Cáceres has routes suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103260130/caceres-monumental-city-and-paseo-alto-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cáceres Monumental City and Paseo Alto Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.7-mile (10.9 km) route that allows you to explore the city's historic areas with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in Cáceres?
A: For more experienced cyclists, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1104170327/sierra-de-fuentes-and-guadiloba-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Fuentes and Guadiloba Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; offer a significant challenge with hilly terrain over 24.9 miles (40.1 km). Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103884442/malpartida-de-caceres-from-caceres-circular-by-monumento-natural-los-barruecos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malpartida de Cáceres from Cáceres – circular by Monumento Natural Los Barruecos&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 21.6 miles (34.8 km) through unique landscapes.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes around Cáceres?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Cáceres are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103884442/malpartida-de-caceres-from-caceres-circular-by-monumento-natural-los-barruecos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malpartida de Cáceres from Cáceres – circular by Monumento Natural Los Barruecos&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103026966/via-de-la-plata-and-embalse-del-casar-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata and Embalse del Casar Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes in Cáceres?
A: Cáceres is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle through areas like the Monfragüe National Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve known for its Mediterranean forests and birdlife. The Jerte Valley offers stunning landscapes with mountains and waterfalls, especially during cherry blossom season. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6093807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Barruecos Natural Monument&lt;/a&gt;, with its unique granite formations and ponds, is also a captivating destination.

Q: Are there historical sites or cultural landmarks to explore on touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes incorporate the rich history of Cáceres. You can cycle through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103260130/caceres-monumental-city-and-paseo-alto-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cáceres Monumental City&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site, with its medieval architecture and Roman and Renaissance structures. The region is also traversed by ancient Roman roads, including parts of the Vía de la Plata, offering a journey through history. Historic towns like Plasencia and Trujillo are also accessible.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Cáceres?
A: The touring cycling routes in Cáceres are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the opportunity to explore historical sites, and the natural beauty of areas like Los Barruecos and the oak forests.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Cáceres?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for touring cycling in Cáceres. Spring brings pleasant temperatures and the spectacular cherry blossoms in the Jerte Valley, while autumn offers vibrant colors in the forests of the Ambroz Valley. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler, so plan accordingly.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of reservoirs or lakes?
A: Yes, several routes provide scenic views of reservoirs and lakes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1104170327/sierra-de-fuentes-and-guadiloba-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Fuentes and Guadiloba Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; passes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4343940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiloba Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103026966/via-de-la-plata-and-embalse-del-casar-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata and Embalse del Casar Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you past the Embalse del Casar reservoir.

Q: How can I find routes that follow ancient Roman roads or historical paths?
A: Cáceres is rich in historical paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103026966/via-de-la-plata-and-embalse-del-casar-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata and Embalse del Casar Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically follows a section of the historic Roman Vía de la Plata. Many other routes in the region also incorporate parts of these ancient roads and pilgrimage paths, connecting towns with rich cultural heritage.

Q: Are there specific regions known for their natural beauty for cycling?
A: Beyond the immediate vicinity of Cáceres, the Jerte Valley in the north is renowned for its natural landscape, including mountains and waterfalls. The Ambroz Valley is another 'natural paradise of contrasts' with high mountains and beautiful rivers, particularly stunning in autumn. The Sierra de Gata also offers lush forests and quaint villages.

Q: Where can I find more information about cycling routes in the Ambroz Valley?
A: For more detailed information on cycling routes specifically within the Ambroz Valley, you can visit the regional tourism website: &lt;a href="https://visitambroz.es/en/rutas-bici-caceres/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitambroz.es&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Being able to walk anywhere through its narrow cobblestone streets is like traveling to another era. You will be surrounded by mansions, Renaissance palaces and churches crowned with stork nests and you will understand why Cáceres is a World Heritage City. Prepare your camera to photograph one of the most beautiful historic centers in Spain.
On the outskirts of town are ruins of old mines and ghost buildings from its industrial past. Bike up to this beautiful spot for incredible views of the city below.

Sierra de Fuentes is a small town on the slopes of the Risco, the highest point of the Sierra de la Mosca, 11 km from the capital. Stop at the town's Plaza España for a coffee and admire the town's church.

The Guadiloba reservoir captures the waters of the Guadiloba river, creating a recreational area and a natural park to support local fauna and migratory birds. Great tracks through the reservoir and the surrounding landscape a short distance from Cáseres.
Gravel track following the curves of the Guadiloba river a short distance from Cáseres. Along the trail, you will pass the ruins of an ancient bridge from Roman times.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Sierra de Fuentes circular route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is approximately 40.1 kilometers (25 miles) long and typically takes around 3 hours and 19 minutes to complete. However, this duration can vary based on your fitness level, the time spent exploring highlights, and the challenging terrain.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as difficult and requires very good fitness. The terrain includes varying surfaces, with some unpaved and difficult sections, particularly around the Valdeflores Mine, where you might need to push your bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Sierra de Fuentes circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and challenging. You'll encounter different surfaces, including a 3 km dirt/gravel road multi-use trail around the Valdeflores Mine. Be prepared for unpaved sections where you might need to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sierra de Fuentes circular route?
faqs.answer: Since the route starts from Cáceres, you can look for parking options within the city or near its outskirts that allow for easy access to the starting point of the circular route. Specific parking for the Valdeflores Mine area is not detailed, but starting from Cáceres provides a good base.

faqs.question: What landmarks or natural features will I see along the Sierra de Fuentes circular route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll pass by several interesting points. These include the historic Historic Centre of Cáceres, the old Valdeflores Mine, the settlement of Sierra de Fuentes, the Guadiloba Reservoir, and the Roman Bridge Over the Guadiloba River. The scenery also features Mediterranean forests, gorges, and granite rock formations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Sierra de Fuentes route?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated for this specific route, the Cáceres region generally offers diverse and picturesque landscapes. Consider visiting during spring or autumn for more moderate temperatures, which are ideal for a challenging touring bicycle ride. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this specific circular route. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Fuentes circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for touring bicycles and includes challenging terrain. While dogs are generally welcome in many outdoor areas, the suitability for dogs on a bicycle route of this difficulty, especially with unpaved sections, depends on the dog's fitness and whether it can safely accompany you (e.g., in a trailer or running alongside on appropriate sections). Always ensure your dog's safety and comfort.

faqs.question: Does this touring cycling route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Camino de los Descubridores - Etapa 1 (15%) and the Vía de la Plata hiking - 02 Merida to Salamanca (4%). It also briefly touches upon the Camino de Cáceres a Sierra de Fuentes (por la umbría) and the Cordel del Casar.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring a well-maintained touring bicycle, appropriate cycling gear, plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit, and navigation tools. Due to unpaved sections, consider tires suitable for mixed surfaces. Dress in layers appropriate for the season.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for navigating the route?
faqs.answer: Be prepared for the 3 km dirt/gravel road section around the Valdeflores Mine. This part is known to be difficult, and you might need to push your bike. Good fitness is essential for the overall 527 meters of elevation gain. Always check local conditions before you go.

waypoints.name: Roman Bridge Over the Guadiloba River
